creator: michael.hmich@gmail.com
text:
now looking from the linux side with parted

hm-lmint %> parted /dev/sda
GNU Parted 3.4
Using /dev/sda
Welcome to GNU Parted! Type 'help' to view a list of commands.
(parted) p                                                                
Model: ATA WDC WD5000AAKX-0 (scsi)
Disk /dev/sda: 500GB
Sector size (logical/physical): 512B/512B
Partition Table: msdos
Disk Flags: 

Number  Start   End     Size    Type      File system     Flags
 1      1049kB  62,9GB  62,9GB  primary   ext4            boot
 2      62,9GB  113GB   50,3GB  extended
 6      62,9GB  70,3GB  7340MB  logical   linux-swap(v1)
 5      70,3GB  113GB   43,0GB  logical   ext4
 3      113GB   480GB   367GB   primary   zfs
 4      480GB   500GB   19,9GB  primary   ext4

which is perfectly working and never gave up any problem for the Linux systems

now back to FreeBSD,
both partitions appeared in dolphin's device list, with a mouse click I could load them ... but are not listed in Kde Setting's Removable Device List

/dev/ada0s1     58G     35G     23G    60%    /media/WDC_WD5000AAKX-003CA0_WD-WCAYUEP01916_s1
/dev/ada0s4     18G     15G    2.3G    86%    /media/WDC_WD5000AAKX-003CA0_WD-WCAYUEP01916_s4

so I guess Kde is knowing how to interpret /etc/fstab partially

/dev/ada0s1             /Linux_Mint             ext2fs          noauto  2       0
/dev/ada0s4             /Kde_Neon               ext2fs          noauto  2       0
/dev/ada0s5             /Arch                   ext2fs          noauto  2       0

but it's also messed up, because ad0s5 is the Arch partition and it is mounted as ada0s4 in dolphin, nevertheless in Dolphin they appear correct mounted as Linux_Mint and Arch
